data.title: Trump Downplays Nominating Hassett as Fed Chair
data.description:
President Trump praised White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett's appearance on Fox News this morning, although another comment caused Hassett's Fed Chair nomination odds to plummet.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I actually want to keep you where you are," Trump said at a White House healthcare roundtable event in a reference to Hassett, adding that he doesn't "want to lose him."

Who Will Trump Nominate as Fed Chair?

Hassett's odds of being nominated by Trump on the prediction platform Polymarket dropped to 15% from 32% following the comment, while former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's odds rose to 60% from 34%. Fed Governor Christopher Waller holds 14% odds.

Trump recently communicated to close associates that he may favor keeping Hassett in place because he appreciates Hassett's defense of his policies on television, according to The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: 'Everything Points to Bitcoin Massively Outperforming Gold,' Says Analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has reached its most significant point of undervaluation relative to gold (CM:XAUUSD) on record, sparking a debate among analysts about a massive price rebound in 2026. This signal, based on the BTC–XAU ratio's Z-score, suggests that Bitcoin is currently trading more than two standard deviations below its historical average compared to the precious metal. Historically, such extreme discounts have been the starting point for some of Bitcoin's most aggressive rallies.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The BTC–XAU Z-Score Hits a Historical Low

To determine if Bitcoin is cheap or expensive compared to gold, traders look at the Z-score of the BTC–XAU ratio. This metric measures how far the current price deviates from the long-term trend. On Friday, the score dropped below −2, entering the lowest band for the first time in the history of the model. Julius, the analyst who conceptualized the Power-Law bands for this ratio, stated that "everything points to Bitcoin massively outperforming Gold over the coming months."

Historical Patterns Point Toward a Triple-Digit Surge

This isn't the first time Bitcoin has looked cheap compared to gold, and the previous outcomes were explosive.

  • November 2022: A similar undervaluation signal was followed by a 150% rally over the next year.
  • March 2020: After hitting this zone during the global liquidity crisis, Bitcoin surged by over 1,170% within 12 months.
  • 2017 Top: Conversely, the Z-score correctly predicted the market peak when Bitcoin became "extremely overbought, while Gold was oversold."

Why Gold's Rally Might Be Good for Bitcoin

Interestingly, the biggest jumps in Bitcoin's price often happen right after gold has finished a major bull run. Historical data indicates that Bitcoin's parabolic growth phases usually lag behind gold's movements by two months to a year. Because gold has spent much of early 2026 at record highs, many investors believe we are entering the window where capital begins to rotate back into digital gold to chase higher returns.

While the current market sentiment remains cautious, several technical models suggest that a return to the fair value mean would be a massive move. If Bitcoin follows the historical pattern of previous gold-led cycles, many analysts project the asset could reach between $200,000 and $300,000 by the end of 2026. However, this depends on whether the historical correlation between these two scarce assets holds firm under current economic conditions.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $94,864.62.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: V, BTC, USDC: Crypto Card Spending Hits $18 Billion
data.description:
Cryptocurrency credit and debit card spending is growing rapidly as digital payments become more mainstream.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Crypto payment cards let users spend stablecoins and other cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) at traditional merchants. New data from Artemis shows that such cards have become one of the fastest-growing segments in the payments space, benefiting companies such as Visa (V).

Monthly crypto card volumes have risen from $100 million in 2023 to more than $1.5 billion at the end of 2025, according to a report by Artemis. The annual market for crypto cards now exceeds $18 billion, nearly matching peer-to-peer stablecoin transfers. Visa's stablecoin card spending reached $3.5 billion in 2025, representing 19% of total crypto card volumes.

Crypto Spending Goes Mainstream

Artemis adds that the crypto card market largely runs on the same rails as traditional Visa and Mastercard (MA) payment networks. However, Visa remains out front in the crypto card market, capturing more than 90% of on-chain card volumes through partnerships with various crypto companies.

The most popular stablecoins linked to crypto cards are USD Coin (USDC), which is administered by Circle Internet Group (CRCL), and Tether's USDT (USDT) coin. However, many crypto cards allow users to spend fractions of Bitcoin or Ethereum. Artemis says it expects continued growth of crypto cards.

Is V Stock a Buy?

Visa's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 26 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 23 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average V price target of $405.00 implies 23.59% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: OpenAI Warns Investors to Prepare for 'Deliberately Outlandish Claims' from Elon Musk
data.description:
Microsoft-backed (MSFT) AI firm OpenAI has warned investors and banking partners to prepare for a lot of drama as its legal battle with Elon Musk moves closer to trial. In a letter sent on Thursday, the company said that it expects Musk to make "deliberately outlandish, attention-grabbing claims" as the case heads to court in April. By issuing this warning in advance, OpenAI appears to be trying to reassure investors and limit uncertainty. This is especially important given the company's impressive growth and $500 billion valuation.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To understand the dispute, it helps to look back at OpenAI's early history and Musk's involvement. Importantly, Musk co-founded OpenAI in 2015 as a nonprofit research lab, alongside several others, including current CEO Sam Altman. However, Musk left OpenAI's board in 2018. Years later, he filed a lawsuit in 2024, claiming that he was misled as OpenAI moved toward a for-profit structure and created a major partnership with Microsoft. Musk has argued that he is owed the value of intellectual property developed after his departure.

As the legal fight heads to court, OpenAI said it has a strong defense and estimates that the case is worth no more than the roughly $38 million Musk donated, though it admitted that there are no guarantees. Nevertheless, OpenAI said it expects Musk to make statements that it views as disconnected from reality, but emphasized that its focus remains on presenting its case clearly to a jury rather than responding publicly.

Is MSFT Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $631.36 per share implies 36.9% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Hassett Shrugs off Powell DOJ Investigation, Says 'Nothing to See Here'
data.description:
White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett minimized the severity of the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Powell on Friday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The bottom line ‌is, I expect, you know, Jay is a good man – I expect that there's nothing to see here, that the cost overruns are related to things like asbestos, as he says," Hassett told Fox News. "But I sure wish they had been more transparent."

Why is Powell Being Investigated?

Several global economic leaders and Fed officials have criticized the investigation, which centers on the Fed's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters and whether the spending was misappropriated. Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr cautioned that the probe is an "assault on Fed independence," while Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said that waning Fed independence could lead to higher inflation.

Earlier this week, Powell framed the investigation as a cover-up for the Fed making monetary policy decisions independently instead of adhering to President Trump's calls for lower rates.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Software Issue": Verizon Stock (NYSE:VZ) Slips as Outage Cause Found, Customers Decry Refund
data.description:
The recent outages suffered by customers of communications stock Verizon (VZ) are proving to be worse news for the company than first glance suggested. The cause of the outage was uncovered, and with it, customers responded to the promise of a $20 credit for the outages suffered. Both of these factors together proved to be a drag on shares, and Verizon shares slipped fractionally in Friday morning's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The outage ran about 10 hours in total, reports note, and hit hundreds of thousands of customers. It was Verizon's first major outage in over a year—the last one was in October 2024—and hit both text and voice calling, unless data use was involved.

But the cause was eventually pinned down and blamed on a "software issue." Verizon issued an update that seemed to resolve the whole thing, and Verizon also noted that a "cyber security issue" was really not to blame here. At least, there were no indications that was the case. Verizon then offered customers a $20 credit to make up for the day they spent without service, but that did not sit well with a lot of customers.

A Paltry Reward

Indeed, customers took issue with the $20 credit, with some subscribers calling the credit "pathetic." Even lawmakers got involved, as Sen. Ben Ray Lujan (D-New Mexico) started work on legislation that would require "pro-rated refunds" by law. Lujan noted, "If you pay for a service, that's what you should be getting."

Verizon, for its part, noted that no credit can really make up for what happened, and the credit would actually account for "…multiple days of service." Though, by the numbers, Verizon is likely correct here—the Unlimited Welcome plan is $65 a month, reports note, so $20 would basically give back over a week's worth of service—Verizon is hard-pressed to account for stories like those of customers unable to reach 911 in an emergency.

Is Verizon Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on VZ stock based on four Buys and nine Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.5% rally in its share price over the past year, the average VZ price target of $46.55 per share implies 19.21% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Spotify Stock (SPOT) Hits a Bum Note Despite Billion Dollar Boost From Price Hikes
data.description:
Shares in music streaming platform Spotify (SPOT) hit a bum note today despite being set for a financial windfall after raising subscription prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spotify is Most Expensive Service

Spotify said this week it would hike the price on U.S. individual premium subscriptions to $12.99 from $11.99, with similar hikes in Estonia and Latvia. The two-person plan is also increasing to $18.99 from $16.99 a month.

It is the first U.S. subscription hike since July 2024, when the Premium tier increased from $10.99 to $11.99. Spotify has also hiked prices in other markets in recent months, such as the U.K., Switzerland and Australia.

Spotify said the increase was necessary "to keep delivering a great experience" and enable the company "to continue offering the best possible experience and benefit artists."

However, it does leave the firm the most expensive streaming service out of peers including Apple (AAPL) Music and Amazon (AMZN) Music.

But instead of that deterring demand and hitting Spotify in the pocket, Evercore ISI tech analyst Mark Mahaney believes it could benefit.

Indeed, he estimates that Spotify could see a 4% to 5% boost in sales assuming the $1 price increase across individual and student plans and a $2 increase for duo and family plans. Assuming the same gross margin profile as the existing business, Mahaney calculated an approximate $270 million boost to gross profit, "most of which should flow down to operating income."

Huge Incremental Increase

In total, Mahaney, who has an Outperform rating on Spotify, calculated an estimated $978 million in incremental revenue from price increases for three quarters of fiscal year 2026.

"We continue to view Spotify as the global leader in streaming audio with accelerating monetization, strong user growth, and improving profitability," Mahaney said. "Average revenue per user expansion is underway, supported by price increases across 150+ markets and steady retention, confirming Spotify's pricing power and low churn. We expect the full uplift to layer in through early 2026 alongside the forthcoming rollout of a higher priced Superfan/Premium+ tier."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Oracle Stock (ORCL) Slips on HQ Hiring Troubles
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) stock was down on Friday alongside news that the multinational technology company is struggling to attract workers to its office in Nashville, TN. This office is planned to be the company's new global headquarters, if Oracle can find employees who actually want to work at the location.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Oracle has reportedly run into trouble hiring employees at the Nashville office for a few reasons. The first is a requirement for on-site work, instead of allowing for remote employees. Another major factor affecting its efforts appears to be pay. While this office would be the company's new HQ, Oracle is reportedly offering employees less compensation compared to what it does at other offices.

The issue that Oracle faces is offering enough that employees will be willing to relocate from other offices. That's a tall order considering the lower pay, even if the cost of living in Nashville is lower. This problem could prevent the company from setting up its new HQ as early as it had hoped. It also might have to rely more on local talent than pulling workers from other areas.

Oracle Stock Movement Today

Oracle stock was down slightly on Friday, extending a 3.15% drop year-to-date. Despite that dip, the stock was still up 17.9% over the past 12 months.

ORCL stock activity today saw some 7.29 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 26.99 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: JPMorgan Stock (JPM) Pops as It Chases Surge in Private Market Deals
data.description:
Shares in U.S. banking giant JPMorgan (JPM) edged higher on Friday after it announced launching a new advisory team to chase private market deals.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The largest American lender said it has created a hybrid team called Private Capital Advisory & Solutions to help businesses raise funds from private markets.

The move comes as investment banks and asset managers alike continue to race to tap into the shift toward fundraising in private markets. For instance, BlackRock (BLK), the world's largset asset manager, is looking to raise $400 billion by 2030 for its private market strategy.

In 2025, new equity issuance in the U.S. rose for the third consecutive year, with 55% more initial public offering (IPO) deals completed in that year, according to S&P Global Market Intelligence (SPGI). However, the aggregate proceeds of $75.5 billion raised by companies from their IPOs is dwarfed by the record $150 billion AI startups raised in the same year, led by heavyweights such as OpenAI and Anthropic.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
